criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 to understand the formal problem formulation: KG G=(V,E), triplets t=(e_h, r, e_t), the objective to extract novel triplets from publications P and integrate them into augmented graph G_new as defined by Equation 1.

The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the system overview of the KARMA multi-agent architecture with nine specialized agents (IA, RA, SA, EEA, REA, SAA, CRA, EA) operating under a Central Controller.
The agent has read Sections 3.3–3.10 to understand the mathematical formulations and roles of each agent, including Equations 2–15.
The agent has read Section 4.1 to understand data collection: 1,200 PubMed articles split into Genomics (720 papers), Proteomics (360 papers), and Metabolomics (120 papers) corpora.

The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand the three LLM backbones used: GLM-4 (9B parameters), GPT-4o (proprietary multimodal), and DeepSeek-v3 (37B activated MoE), all accessed via APIs, with DeepSeek-v3 used for all LLM-based evaluations.
The agent has read Section 4.3 to understand all evaluation metrics: Core Metrics (M_Con, M_Cla, M_Rel), Graph Statistics (Delta_Cov, Delta_Con), and Quality Indicators (R_CR, R_LC, C_QA, R_HE).
The agent has read Table 1 to understand the main evaluation results across domains and models, including DeepSeek-v3 achieving Delta_Cov=38,230 and R_LC=0.831 in genomics.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the ablation study results, evaluating KARMA-Full against w/o Summarizer Agent, w/o Conflict Resolution Agent, and w/o Evaluator Agent configurations.
The agent has read Appendix B to understand the detailed prompt templates for each KARMA agent (IA, RA, SA, EEA, REA, SAA, CRA, EA for confidence/clarity/relevance).
The plan includes implementing a KG data structure with entity set V, edge set E, and triplet representation t=(e_h, r, e_t) compatible with the augmented graph G_new formulation.
The plan includes a Central Controller to orchestrate the nine KARMA agents in a hierarchical multi-agent pipeline.

The plan includes implementing all nine specialized agents: Ingestion (IA), Reader (RA), Summarizer (SA), Entity Extraction (EEA), Relationship Extraction (REA), Schema Alignment (SAA), Conflict Resolution (CRA), and three Evaluator Agents (EA for confidence, clarity, relevance).
The plan includes using three LLM backbones (GLM-4, GPT-4o, DeepSeek-v3) via their respective APIs, with each experiment sharing the same backbone across all agents.
The plan includes a single-agent baseline that extracts all triples in a single LLM generation without the multi-agent pipeline.
The plan includes implementation of all nine evaluation metrics: M_Con, M_Cla, M_Rel, Delta_Cov, Delta_Con, R_CR, R_LC, C_QA, and R_HE.
The plan includes an ablation study with four configurations: KARMA-Full, w/o Summarizer Agent, w/o Conflict Resolution Agent, and w/o Evaluator Agent, all using DeepSeek-v3.
The code implements the KG data structure as G=(V, E) with entities V and directed edges E, supporting triplets t=(e_h, r, e_t) and augmented graph G_new via Equation 1.

The Ingestion Agent (IA) is implemented to normalize raw publications (handling OCR errors, structural inconsistencies) and extract metadata (journal, date, authors), outputting a standardized text plus metadata as defined by Equation 2.

The Reader Agent (RA) is implemented to segment normalized documents into sections and assign a relevance score R(s_j) = LLM_reader(s_j, G) per Equation 3, filtering out segments below domain-calibrated threshold delta.

The Summarizer Agent (SA) is implemented to condense each segment s_j into a concise summary u_j = LLM_summ(s_j, P_summ) per Equation 4, retaining critical entities, relations, and domain-specific terms.

The Entity Extraction Agent (EEA) is implemented with LLM-based NER E(u_j) = LLM_E(u_j, P_E) ⊙ D_E per Equation 5, applying dictionary/ontology-based filtering to reduce false positives and normalize entity mentions.

Entity normalization is implemented via Equation 6: mapping raw entity e to normalized entity ê = argmin_{v in V} d(phi(e), psi(v)) in a joint embedding space, with entities exceeding threshold rho flagged as new candidates V+.

The Relationship Extraction Agent (REA) is implemented using LLM-based multi-label classification p(r | ê_i, ê_j, u_j) = LLM_R(ê_i, ê_j, u_j, P_R) per Equations 7-8, selecting relationships with probability >= threshold theta_r.

The Schema Alignment Agent (SAA) is implemented via Equation 9 to classify new entities v into valid types tau* = argmax_{tau in T} LLM_SAA(v, tau, P_align) and map new relations to KG relation types, flagging unmatched entities/relations as candidate additions.

The Conflict Resolution Agent (CRA) is implemented with LLM-based debate per Equations 10-11: detecting conflicting triplets in G and outputting {Agree, Contradict}, discarding or queuing contradicting triplets for expert review.
The Evaluator Agent (EA) is implemented to compute confidence C(t) = sigma(sum alpha_i v_i(t)) per Equation 12 using weighted verification signals with logistic activation.
The Evaluator Agent computes clarity Cl(t) = sigma(sum beta_j c_j(t)) per Equation 13 and relevance R(t) = sigma(sum gamma_k r_k(t)) per Equation 14.
The final integration decision is implemented as Equation 15: integrate(t)=1 if (C(t)+Cl(t)+R(t))/3 >= Theta, otherwise 0, controlling which triplets enter the KG.
The Central Controller is implemented to orchestrate all nine agents in the correct pipeline order: IA → RA → SA → EEA/REA → SAA → CRA → EA → KG integration.
The data collection pipeline is implemented to ingest 1,200 PubMed articles split across Genomics (720), Proteomics (360), and Metabolomics (120) corpora from PDF format.
LLM backbone integrations are implemented for GLM-4, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-v3 via their respective APIs, with each agent using the same backbone per experiment run.
The single-agent baseline is implemented to extract all triples from each document in a single LLM generation without the multi-agent pipeline stages.

Coverage Gain (Delta_Cov) is implemented as the count of newly introduced entities not previously in the KG, and Connectivity Gain (Delta_Con) is computed as the net increase in node degrees over existing entities.
Conflict Ratio (R_CR) is implemented as the fraction of newly extracted edges removed by the Conflict Resolution Agent due to contradictions.
LLM-based Correctness (R_LC) is implemented: a hold-out LLM judges each new triple as likely correct, uncertain, or likely incorrect, with R_LC = #(likely correct) / #(all new triples).
QA Coherence (C_QA) is implemented to evaluate the fraction of plausible KG-derived answers for a curated set of domain-specific questions answerable via KG traversal.
Human Evaluation Score (R_HE) collection is implemented, with assessments from two human experts rating the quality of triple extractions on a 0-to-1 scale.

Ablation configurations are implemented: w/o Summarizer (bypass SA, pass raw RA output to EEA/REA), w/o Conflict Resolution (skip CRA), and w/o Evaluator (skip EA, integrate all triplets without filtering).

The agent-specific prompt templates from Appendix B are implemented for each of the nine agents (IA_Prompt, RA_Prompt, SA_Prompt, EEA_Prompt, REA_Prompt, SAA_Prompt, CRA_Prompt, EA_Prompt_confidence, EA_Prompt_clarity, EA_Prompt_relevance).

The main experiments are executed with all three LLM backbones (GLM-4, GPT-4o, DeepSeek-v3) and the single-agent baseline across all three domains (Genomics, Proteomics, Metabolomics), producing all metrics in Table 1.

The ablation study is executed using DeepSeek-v3 across all three domains with all four configurations (KARMA-Full, w/o Summarizer, w/o Conflict Resolution, w/o Evaluator), producing results for Table 2.
The cost analysis is executed to measure prompt tokens, completion tokens, and processing time per domain, producing the distributions shown in Figure 3.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: KARMA with DeepSeek-v3 achieves Delta_Cov=38,230 new entities in genomics, representing 3.9× higher coverage gain than GPT-4o in the same domain.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: DeepSeek-v3 achieves R_LC=0.831 in genomics while GLM-4 achieves R_LC=0.623 and GPT-4o achieves R_LC=0.880.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: KARMA multi-agent framework consistently outperforms the single-agent baseline across all three domains on R_LC and C_QA metrics.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: DeepSeek-v3 achieves superior performance on 17 out of 24 metrics across all three domains.

The execution outcomes match Table 1: In proteomics, DeepSeek-v3 achieves M_Con=0.845, Delta_Con=1.468, and R_LC=0.772; in metabolomics, DeepSeek-v3 achieves Delta_Cov=1,752 which is 127% higher than GPT-4o.
The ablation study outcomes match Table 2: removing the Summarizer Agent causes C_QA to drop by 22.9% (0.612→0.472) in genomics and R_LC to drop by 18.2% (0.772→0.632) in proteomics.

The ablation study outcomes match Table 2: removing the Conflict Resolution Agent reduces R_LC from 0.831 to 0.790 (-4.9%) in genomics, and removing the Evaluator Agent reduces R_LC from 0.668 to 0.603 (-9.7%) in metabolomics.

The cost analysis outcomes match Figure 3: genomics shows higher completion token distributions (mean=550.64, std=232.92) and proteomics shows broader processing time distributions (mean=96.58, std=46.90).
